Bash(cd D:/Himanshu/dev/StreamlinePRO && gh pr create --title "feat\(tasks\): add recurrence management UI" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Add 5 server actions \(create, update, pause, resume, clear\) for managing task recurrence from the drawer
- Add `RecurrenceCard` server component with three states: disabled \(subtask/completed/no due date\), empty \(cadence controls\), and active \(summary + edit/pause/remove\)
- Add due-date clearing guard on `updateTaskAction` to prevent removing due dates from recurring tasks
- Thread `recurrenceEditorState` through task drawer loader and both page files \(`/my-tasks`, `/projects/[projectId]`\)

## New files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `lib/validators/recurrence.ts` | Zod schemas for all 5 actions |
| `lib/domain/tasks/recurrence-types.ts` | Shared types \(`TaskRecurrenceSummary`, `TaskRecurrenceEditorState`\) |
| `lib/domain/tasks/recurrence-state.ts` | `resolveRecurrenceEditorState\(\)` — eligibility + summary builder |
| `lib/actions/recurrence-actions.ts` | 5 `'use server'` CRUD actions |
| `app/components/tasks/recurrence-card.tsx` | Server component rendered in task drawer |
| `tests/unit/validators-recurrence.test.ts` | 13 validator tests |
| `tests/unit/actions/recurrence-actions.test.ts` | 11 action tests |
| `tests/unit/domain/recurrence-state.test.ts` | 8 state resolver tests |

## Test plan

- [x] All 231 unit tests pass \(`vitest run`\)
- [x] Zero ESLint violations \(`next lint`\)
- [ ] Open top-level task with due date → empty recurrence card with cadence controls
- [ ] Set recurrence → card shows summary \(e.g. "Every 2 weeks"\) + next due preview
- [ ] Pause → paused badge, complete task → no new task spawned
- [ ] Resume → badge gone, complete task → new task with advanced due date
- [ ] Remove recurrence → card returns to empty state
- [ ] Open subtask → disabled card: "Subtasks cannot have recurrence"
- [ ] Open task without due date → disabled: "Set a due date first"
- [ ] Try clearing due date on recurring task → error message

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)
EOF
\)")
